name: Release desktop

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - '@lightfast/desktop@*'

concurrency:
  group: desktop-release-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  prepare:
    name: Prepare draft release
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}
      tag: ${{ steps.ver.outputs.tag }}
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Resolve version
        id: ver
        run: |
          tag="${{ github.ref_name }}"
          echo "tag=$tag" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "version=${tag#@lightfast/desktop@}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Create draft release if missing
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          if gh release view "${{ steps.ver.outputs.tag }}" --repo "${{ github.repository }}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "Release already exists, reusing."
          else
            prev=$(git tag -l '@lightfast/desktop@*' --sort=-v:refname | grep -v "${{ steps.ver.outputs.tag }}" | head -n1 || true)
            notes_arg=(--generate-notes)
            if [ -n "$prev" ]; then
              notes_arg=(--generate-notes --notes-start-tag "$prev")
            fi
            gh release create "${{ steps.ver.outputs.tag }}" \
              --repo "${{ github.repository }}" \
              --draft \
              --title "Lightfast desktop v${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}" \
              "${notes_arg[@]}"
          fi

  build:
    name: Build ${{ matrix.arch }}
    needs: prepare
    runs-on: macos-14
    timeout-minutes: 60
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        arch: [arm64, x64]
    permissions:
      contents: write
      id-token: write
      attestations: write
    env:
      GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY: ${{ secrets.APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY }}
      APPLE_TEAM_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_TEAM_ID }}
      APPLE_API_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_API_KEY_ID }}
      APPLE_API_ISSUER: ${{ secrets.APPLE_API_ISSUER }}
      SENTRY_DSN: ${{ secrets.SENTRY_DSN }}
      SENTRY_ORG: ${{ vars.SENTRY_ORG }}
      SENTRY_PROJECT: ${{ vars.SENTRY_PROJECT }}
      SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js 22
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: 'pnpm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Stamp version + feed URL in package.json
        working-directory: apps/desktop
        run: |
          npm version "${{ needs.prepare.outputs.version }}" --no-git-tag-version --allow-same-version
          npm pkg set buildFlavor=prod
          npm pkg set buildNumber="$GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER"
          npm pkg set sparkleFeedUrl='https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/releases/latest/download/latest-mac-${arch}.json'

      - name: Import Apple signing certificate
        env:
          APPLE_CERT_BASE64: ${{ secrets.APPLE_CERT_BASE64 }}
          APPLE_CERT_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.APPLE_CERT_PASSWORD }}
          KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD }}
        run: |
          echo "$APPLE_CERT_BASE64" | base64 -d > /tmp/cert.p12
          security create-keychain -p "$KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD" build.keychain
          security default-keychain -s build.keychain
          security unlock-keychain -p "$KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD" build.keychain
          security set-keychain-settings -t 3600 -u build.keychain
          security import /tmp/cert.p12 -k build.keychain -P "$APPLE_CERT_PASSWORD" -T /usr/bin/codesign
          security set-key-partition-list -S apple-tool:,apple:,codesign: -s -k "$KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD" build.keychain
          rm /tmp/cert.p12

      - name: Write notarize API key
        env:
          APPLE_API_KEY_CONTENT: ${{ secrets.APPLE_API_KEY_CONTENT }}
          APPLE_API_KEY_ID_SECRET: ${{ secrets.APPLE_API_KEY_ID }}
        run: |
          mkdir -p "$HOME/.private_keys"
          key_file="$HOME/.private_keys/AuthKey_${APPLE_API_KEY_ID_SECRET}.p8"
          printf '%s' "$APPLE_API_KEY_CONTENT" > "$key_file"
          echo "APPLE_API_KEY=$key_file" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"

      - name: Publish (forge)
        working-directory: apps/desktop
        run: pnpm exec electron-forge publish --arch=${{ matrix.arch }} --platform=darwin

      - name: Upload source maps to Sentry
        working-directory: apps/desktop
        run: pnpm sourcemaps:upload

      - name: Attest build provenance
        uses: actions/attest-build-provenance@v2
        with:
          subject-path: 'apps/desktop/out/make/**/*.zip'

  finalize:
    name: Finalize release
    needs: [prepare, build]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Generate Squirrel.Mac update feed
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          GH_REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.prepare.outputs.tag }}
          RELEASE_VERSION: ${{ needs.prepare.outputs.version }}
        run: node apps/desktop/scripts/generate-update-feed.mjs

      - name: Publish release (undraft)
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          gh release edit "${{ needs.prepare.outputs.tag }}" \
            --repo "${{ github.repository }}" \
            --draft=false

11	---
12	
13	# Desktop Pre-Release Batch Implementation Plan
14	
15	## Status (2026-04-24)
16	
17	**Phases B–F shipped via PR #621** (merge commit `aef1d3240`). All code/CI changes called for by the plan are on `main`. The release pipeline is wired but inert — it activates on the first `@lightfast/desktop@*` tag.
18	
19	### What landed
20	
21	- **Phase B — Env layer overhaul.** `apps/desktop/src/env/main.ts` + `env/renderer.ts` (t3-env + Zod), old `parseRuntimeEnv` / `runtimeEnvSchema` / `src/shared/env.ts` removed, all consumers migrated to `mainEnv` / `rendererEnv`. `SENTRY_DSN` baked via Vite `define` with Sentry's custom-token pattern (`__SENTRY_DSN__`). `sparklePublicKey` dropped end-to-end (package.json, build-info, IPC snapshot).
22	- **Phase C — Sentry source-map upload script.** `apps/desktop/scripts/upload-sourcemaps.mjs` + `pnpm sourcemaps:upload`, `@sentry/cli` devDep. Uploads both `.vite/build` and `.vite/renderer/main_window` with `--url-prefix app:///` to match `rewriteFramesIntegration`.
23	- **Phase D — `desktop-release.yml` enabled + hardened.** Tag trigger `@lightfast/desktop@*`, `cancel-in-progress`, arm64+x64 matrix, Apple codesign + notarize + `actions/attest-build-provenance@v2`, auto-generated release notes from the previous matching tag. Workflow stays inert until a tag is cut.
24	- **Phase E — `desktop-ci.yml` added.** Native `paths:` filter on desktop-touching paths; typecheck + unsigned `electron-forge package` on `macos-14`. Later pinned `pnpm/action-setup` to a commit SHA and added top-level `permissions: contents: read` in response to CodeQL findings.
25	- **Phase F — Contributor ergonomics.** `apps/desktop/.env.example`, `clean` script, `@lightfast/desktop` added to `.changeset/config.json.ignore`. Originally renamed `dev:desktop-stack` → `dev:desktop-api`, but that rename was superseded before merge by main commit `f1786701d`: Turbo 2.9's built-in microfrontends integration now owns the proxy task, so the wrapper script was removed entirely. `pnpm dev:full` boots the proxy at 3024 alongside the Next.js apps.
26	- **Follow-on (not in the original plan).** Wired `dotenv-cli` + `with-env` script so `pnpm dev:desktop` loads `apps/desktop/.vercel/.env.development.local` into the main process — fills a gap the plan assumed existed but nothing actually did the wiring. Mirrors the `apps/app` pattern.
27	
28	### Verification performed
29	
30	**Automated (all green):**
31	- `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` + `pnpm check` (biome/ultracite).
32	- `SENTRY_DSN='https://fake@sentry.io/12345' pnpm package` inlines the literal DSN into `.vite/build`; unset DSN leaves zero `__SENTRY_DSN__` tokens.
33	- Missing `NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY` produces t3-env's banner naming the exact variable.
34	- Sourcemap-upload script (with mocked `sentry-cli`) makes 4 calls in expected order: `releases new` → two `upload-sourcemaps` with `--url-prefix app:///` → `releases finalize`.
35	- Desktop CI `paths:` filter simulated locally with `picomatch`: 12/12 cases correct (desktop + packages-{app-trpc,ui,lib} + pnpm-lock trigger; www/app/platform/other-workflows skip).
36	- `desktop-release.yml` structural invariants (trigger, concurrency, matrix, env surfaces, permissions, sourcemap + attestation steps, prev-tag notes): all verified via `yaml`-parser script.
37	- CI on PR #621: 15/15 checks passed, including CodeQL + CodeRabbit review. Two CodeQL alerts raised and resolved (`actions/unpinned-tag` + `actions/missing-workflow-permissions` — both on `desktop-ci.yml`). CodeRabbit raised one blocker (README `.env.development` vs `.vercel/.env.development.local` path mismatch) — fixed in `20c56310f`.
38	
39	**Interactive (end-to-end sign-in):** With `apps/{app,platform,www}/.vercel/.env.development.local` copied into a worktree, ran `pnpm dev:desktop-api` + `pnpm dev:desktop`. Clerk sign-in succeeded → loopback callback accepted → `GET /api/trpc/account.get 200` proved the renderer was authenticated. No errors from our code in the desktop or API logs.
40	
41	### Left for a human (out of scope for PR #621)
42	
43	- **Phase A (Apple + Sentry secrets).** Not yet authorized by Apple. Workflow is inert until the 8 Apple secrets (`APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY`, `APPLE_TEAM_ID`, `APPLE_CERT_BASE64`, `APPLE_CERT_PASSWORD`, `APPLE_API_KEY_ID`, `APPLE_API_ISSUER`, `APPLE_API_KEY_CONTENT`, `KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD`) + 2 Sentry secrets (`SENTRY_DSN`, `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN`) + 2 Sentry vars (`SENTRY_ORG`, `SENTRY_PROJECT`) are provisioned.
44	- **Phase D dry-run.** After Phase A lands, cut `@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0-rc.1` and verify codesign/notarize/attestation/feed JSON end-to-end.
45	- **Phase E branch protection.** Add `Desktop CI / Typecheck + package (unsigned)` as a required status check on `main` (repo-settings UI; not automatable).
46	- **Phase B/C manual UX checks.** Real-Sentry artifact verification in the Sentry UI, and fresh-contributor onboarding from `.env.example`.
47	
48	---
49	
50	## Overview
51	
52	Get `apps/desktop` from "quick-wins applied" to "first public `@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0` release shipping a signed, notarized, auto-updating macOS binary with Sentry-readable stack traces." Six phases. Phase A is a human-setup checklist; B–F are code/CI changes. All four prior quick-win phases are already applied and verified in the current tree.
53	
54	## Current State Analysis
55	
56	All four phases of `2026-04-23-desktop-codex-gap-quick-wins.md` are landed:
57	- Entitlements trimmed (`build/entitlements.mac.plist` has 5 keys; over-broad `disable-library-validation`/`device.camera`/`network.server` removed).
58	- `forge.config.ts:67-78` has `NSQuitAlwaysKeepsWindows=false`, `LSMinimumSystemVersion=12.0`, `LSEnvironment.MallocNanoZone="0"`.
59	- `src/main/sentry.ts:14-55` has `SESSION_ID`, `rewriteFramesIntegration({root, prefix: "app:///"})`, `dist=buildNumber`, `bundle/host/sessionId` tags.
60	- `showContextMenu` IPC, `silentRefresh`, and the deep-link `console.log` are gone. `protocol.ts` deleted; OAuth moved to a loopback HTTP server (`auth-flow.ts:70-141`).
61	
62	What still blocks a v0.1.0 release:
63	- `.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml.disabled` is inert (correctly, since Apple signing secrets aren't provisioned yet).
64	- `forge.config.ts:43-54` reads `LIGHTFAST_DESKTOP_RELEASE_REPO` but the disabled workflow passes `${{ github.repository }}` — two conflicting paths.
65	- `@sentry/cli` is in `pnpm-workspace.yaml:82` `onlyBuiltDependencies` but no script invokes it; packaged-build stack traces will be unreadable despite `rewriteFramesIntegration` being wired.
66	- `ci-core.yml:45,105` explicitly excludes desktop from typecheck + build. `ci.yml:50-54` only runs typecheck via `turbo --affected`. Nothing runs `electron-forge package` on PRs.
67	- `apps/desktop/package.json` placeholders: `version: "0.0.0"`, empty `sparkleFeedUrl`, unused `sparklePublicKey: ""`.
68	- No `.env.example`, no `lint`/`clean` scripts in desktop, `dev:desktop-stack` is misnamed at the root.
69	
70	### Key Discoveries
71	
72	- `sparklePublicKey` is declared in `src/shared/env.ts:12`, `src/shared/ipc.ts:52`, and `src/main/build-info.ts:23` but has **zero** downstream readers (grep confirms). Electron's `autoUpdater` does not use it. Drop the field instead of stamping it in CI.
73	- Sentry DSNs are public identifiers (they're visible in any shipped app's JS bundle). Baking at CI build time via `npm pkg set sentryDsn=...` from a secret is simplest — no runtime env plumbing required on macOS.
74	- Release tag convention in this repo is already `<package>@<version>` (changesets produces `lightfast@x.y.z`, `@lightfastai/mcp@x.y.z` via `release.yml`). Desktop should match: `@lightfast/desktop@<version>`. YAML trigger: `'@lightfast/desktop@*'`.
75	- Desktop is `"private": true`; `changesets/action` ignores private packages automatically — it cannot and will not try to publish desktop. The blocker isn't publishing, it's that `verify-changeset.yml:51` rejects any changeset file mentioning `@lightfast/desktop`. We stay tag-only and add the package to `.changeset/config.json` `ignore` so `pnpm changeset` never prompts for it.
76	- Monorepo is public (`gh repo view` → `"visibility":"PUBLIC"`). Sparkle feed URL `github.com/lightfastai/lightfast/releases/latest/download/latest-mac-${arch}.json` works without auth.
77	- `.vite/build` (main + preload) and `.vite/renderer/main_window` (renderer) are the two source-map roots `sentry-cli upload-sourcemaps --url-prefix "app:///"` needs to target — both bundles end up addressed via the `app:///` prefix by `rewriteFramesIntegration`.
78	
79	## Desired End State
80	
81	1. `git tag @lightfast/desktop@0.1.0 && git push origin '@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0'` fires `desktop-release.yml`, which builds signed+notarized arm64 and x64 `.zip` + `.dmg`, uploads source maps to Sentry, publishes GitHub release assets with Squirrel.Mac JSON feeds, and undrafts the release.
82	2. Packaged app's Sentry issues have fully symbolicated stacks (file/line → repo file/line), session tag, `dist`, and the `@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0+<runNumber>` release id.
83	3. Every PR touching `apps/desktop/**` runs typecheck + `electron-forge package` on macOS and fails if either breaks.
84	4. A new contributor runs `cp apps/desktop/.env.example apps/desktop/.env.development && pnpm dev:desktop` and the app boots.
85	5. `pnpm changeset` in the repo never prompts for `@lightfast/desktop`.
86	
87	Verification:
88	- `gh release view '@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0' --repo lightfastai/lightfast` shows 6 assets (arm64 zip+dmg+json, x64 zip+dmg+json) plus undraft state.
89	- `sentry-cli releases info '@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0+<runNumber>' --project lightfast-desktop` lists uploaded source maps.
90	- `.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml` exists and is green on the tag push.
91	- `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop lint` passes.
92	
93	## What We're NOT Doing
94	
95	- Sparkle-native / Ed25519 auto-update (Squirrel.Mac is fine for v0.1.0). `sparklePublicKey` gets deleted, not stamped.
96	- Windows + Linux packaging (`MakerSquirrel` stays in `forge.config.ts:82-85` dormant; no Linux makers; no Windows job in the workflow).
97	- Universal binaries (`@electron/universal`). Two separate `-arm64` / `-x64` artifacts are published; the existing `${arch}` template in `updater.ts:27-29` handles dispatch.
98	- File-backed structured logger. Sentry + breadcrumbs cover v0.1.0; revisit once a user-issue-debugging workflow demands it.
99	- SQLite / persistent structured storage, worker tier, per-surface preload isolation, renderer i18n, Playwright harness, notifications — all deferred per original research `Status Tracker`.
100	- Wiring desktop into the changeset-driven release flow (`release.yml`). Desktop stays tag-only.
101	- A cross-repo `lightfastai/lightfast-desktop-releases` release host. Monorepo tags only.
102	
103	## Implementation Approach
104	
105	Strict phase order because of dependencies:
106	
107	- **Phase A (setup)** must happen before D can be tested end-to-end. Runs in parallel with B/C/E/F code work.
108	- **Phase B** must land before D (D depends on Vite's DSN injection from B).
109	- **Phase C** adds the script that D calls.
110	- **Phases E and F** are independent — can land in either order relative to B–D.
111	
112	### Prerequisites (outside this plan)
113	
114	- `thoughts/shared/plans/2026-04-24-coderabbit-pr614-fixes.md` — resolve the port-collision and `will-navigate` guard items flagged against the loopback OAuth flow in PR #614 before Phase D's end-to-end validation. An unsigned release that crashes on a port conflict during sign-in will look worse than not releasing at all.
115	
116	---
117	
118	## Phase A: External Setup Checklist (human)
119	
120	### Overview
121	
122	No code. Provision the four external resources the workflow depends on, and commit secrets to GitHub. Skipping any item here will leave `desktop-release.yml` failing on the first run.
123	
124	### Apple Developer (8 secrets)
125	
126	1. Enroll in **Apple Developer Program** (~$99/yr) under the Lightfast business entity. Capture the 10-character **Team ID** once enrolled.
127	2. Generate a **Developer ID Application** certificate in developer.apple.com → Certificates. Download the `.cer`, add to Keychain Access on a Mac, export the matching identity as a password-protected `.p12`.
128	3. Generate an **App Store Connect API key** with "Developer" role (developer.apple.com → Users and Access → Keys). Download the `.p8` (one-time download). Capture the **Key ID** (10-char) and **Issuer ID** (UUID).
129	4. In repo settings → Secrets and variables → Actions, add these secrets:
130	   - `APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY` — full identity string, e.g. `Developer ID Application: Lightfast AI Inc (ABC1234567)`
131	   - `APPLE_TEAM_ID` — 10-char team id (e.g. `ABC1234567`)
132	   - `APPLE_CERT_BASE64` — output of `base64 < DeveloperID.p12 | pbcopy` (macOS) or `base64 -w0 DeveloperID.p12` (Linux)
133	   - `APPLE_CERT_PASSWORD` — the p12 password chosen at export
134	   - `APPLE_API_KEY_ID` — the 10-char key id from step 3
135	   - `APPLE_API_ISSUER` — the issuer UUID from step 3
136	   - `APPLE_API_KEY_CONTENT` — raw contents of the `.p8` file (including the `-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----` header/footer)
137	   - `KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD` — any strong random string (e.g. `openssl rand -base64 24`); used only inside the CI keychain
138	
139	### Sentry (2 secrets + 1 project)
140	
141	1. In Sentry, create a new **Electron** project named `lightfast-desktop` under the existing Lightfast org. Capture the DSN.
142	2. Create a Sentry **organization auth token** with `project:releases` scope (Settings → Developer Settings → Auth Tokens).
143	3. Add these secrets to the repo:
144	   - `SENTRY_DSN` — the DSN from step 1 (public, but storing as a secret keeps it out of git)
145	   - `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` — the auth token from step 2
146	4. Add these repo variables (Variables tab, not Secrets):
147	   - `SENTRY_ORG` — Sentry org slug (e.g. `lightfast`)
148	   - `SENTRY_PROJECT` — `lightfast-desktop`
149	
150	### Success Criteria
151	
152	Nothing to automate. Cross off the 11 items above; confirm by visiting `github.com/lightfastai/lightfast/settings/secrets/actions` and seeing all 8 Apple secrets + 2 Sentry secrets + 2 Sentry variables present.
153	
154	**Stop here for human confirmation that Phase A is complete before starting Phase D validation.**
155	
156	---
157	
158	## Phase B: Env layer overhaul + forge config + sparkle cleanup
159	
160	### Overview
161	
162	Four cleanups, with the env layer as the load-bearing change:
163	
164	1. **New t3-env-based env layer** at `apps/desktop/src/env/` — replaces the silent-fail `parseRuntimeEnv` in `src/shared/env.ts` with two `createEnv` calls (main, renderer). Matches the repo convention used by every other workspace.
165	2. **Sentry DSN baked via Vite `define` with a custom token** (`__SENTRY_DSN__`) — not via `process.env.SENTRY_DSN` replacement. Sentry's official Electron troubleshooting docs warn against replacing `process.*` keys in Vite `define` because it can break Sentry SDK internals. Custom token pattern avoids the conflict.
166	3. **Forge config simplification** — hardcode the release repo, remove the `LIGHTFAST_DESKTOP_RELEASE_REPO` indirection.
167	4. **Sparkle cleanup** — drop the unused `sparklePublicKey` field end-to-end.
168

218	      .number()
219	      .int()
220	      .min(1)
221	      .max(65535)
222	      .optional(),
223	  },

226	      typeof __SENTRY_DSN__ !== "undefined" && __SENTRY_DSN__ !== ""
227	        ? __SENTRY_DSN__

# Desktop Pre-Release Batch Implementation Plan

## Status (2026-04-24)

**Phases B–F shipped via PR #621** (merge commit `aef1d3240`). All code/CI changes called for by the plan are on `main`. The release pipeline is wired but inert — it activates on the first `@lightfast/desktop@*` tag.

### What landed

- **Phase B — Env layer overhaul.** `apps/desktop/src/env/main.ts` + `env/renderer.ts` (t3-env + Zod), old `parseRuntimeEnv` / `runtimeEnvSchema` / `src/shared/env.ts` removed, all consumers migrated to `mainEnv` / `rendererEnv`. `SENTRY_DSN` baked via Vite `define` with Sentry's custom-token pattern (`__SENTRY_DSN__`). `sparklePublicKey` dropped end-to-end (package.json, build-info, IPC snapshot).
- **Phase C — Sentry source-map upload script.** `apps/desktop/scripts/upload-sourcemaps.mjs` + `pnpm sourcemaps:upload`, `@sentry/cli` devDep. Uploads both `.vite/build` and `.vite/renderer/main_window` with `--url-prefix app:///` to match `rewriteFramesIntegration`.
- **Phase D — `desktop-release.yml` enabled + hardened.** Tag trigger `@lightfast/desktop@*`, `cancel-in-progress`, arm64+x64 matrix, Apple codesign + notarize + `actions/attest-build-provenance@v2`, auto-generated release notes from the previous matching tag. Workflow stays inert until a tag is cut.
- **Phase E — `desktop-ci.yml` added.** Native `paths:` filter on desktop-touching paths; typecheck + unsigned `electron-forge package` on `macos-14`. Later pinned `pnpm/action-setup` to a commit SHA and added top-level `permissions: contents: read` in response to CodeQL findings.
- **Phase F — Contributor ergonomics.** `apps/desktop/.env.example`, `clean` script, `@lightfast/desktop` added to `.changeset/config.json.ignore`. Originally renamed `dev:desktop-stack` → `dev:desktop-api`, but that rename was superseded before merge by main commit `f1786701d`: Turbo 2.9's built-in microfrontends integration now owns the proxy task, so the wrapper script was removed entirely. `pnpm dev:full` boots the proxy at 3024 alongside the Next.js apps.
- **Follow-on (not in the original plan).** Wired `dotenv-cli` + `with-env` script so `pnpm dev:desktop` loads `apps/desktop/.vercel/.env.development.local` into the main process — fills a gap the plan assumed existed but nothing actually did the wiring. Mirrors the `apps/app` pattern.

### Verification performed

**Automated (all green):**
- `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` + `pnpm check` (biome/ultracite).
- `SENTRY_DSN='https://fake@sentry.io/12345' pnpm package` inlines the literal DSN into `.vite/build`; unset DSN leaves zero `__SENTRY_DSN__` tokens.
- Missing `NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY` produces t3-env's banner naming the exact variable.
- Sourcemap-upload script (with mocked `sentry-cli`) makes 4 calls in expected order: `releases new` → two `upload-sourcemaps` with `--url-prefix app:///` → `releases finalize`.
- Desktop CI `paths:` filter simulated locally with `picomatch`: 12/12 cases correct (desktop + packages-{app-trpc,ui,lib} + pnpm-lock trigger; www/app/platform/other-workflows skip).
- `desktop-release.yml` structural invariants (trigger, concurrency, matrix, env surfaces, permissions, sourcemap + attestation steps, prev-tag notes): all verified via `yaml`-parser script.
- CI on PR #621: 15/15 checks passed, including CodeQL + CodeRabbit review. Two CodeQL alerts raised and resolved (`actions/unpinned-tag` + `actions/missing-workflow-permissions` — both on `desktop-ci.yml`). CodeRabbit raised one blocker (README `.env.development` vs `.vercel/.env.development.local` path mismatch) — fixed in `20c56310f`.

**Interactive (end-to-end sign-in):** With `apps/{app,platform,www}/.vercel/.env.development.local` copied into a worktree, ran `pnpm dev:desktop-api` + `pnpm dev:desktop`. Clerk sign-in succeeded → loopback callback accepted → `GET /api/trpc/account.get 200` proved the renderer was authenticated. No errors from our code in the desktop or API logs.

### Left for a human (out of scope for PR #621)

- **Phase A (Apple + Sentry secrets).** Not yet authorized by Apple. Workflow is inert until the 8 Apple secrets (`APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY`, `APPLE_TEAM_ID`, `APPLE_CERT_BASE64`, `APPLE_CERT_PASSWORD`, `APPLE_API_KEY_ID`, `APPLE_API_ISSUER`, `APPLE_API_KEY_CONTENT`, `KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD`) + 2 Sentry secrets (`SENTRY_DSN`, `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN`) + 2 Sentry vars (`SENTRY_ORG`, `SENTRY_PROJECT`) are provisioned.
- **Phase D dry-run.** After Phase A lands, cut `@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0-rc.1` and verify codesign/notarize/attestation/feed JSON end-to-end.
- **Phase E branch protection.** Add `Desktop CI / Typecheck + package (unsigned)` as a required status check on `main` (repo-settings UI; not automatable).
- **Phase B/C manual UX checks.** Real-Sentry artifact verification in the Sentry UI, and fresh-contributor onboarding from `.env.example`.

---

## Overview

Get `apps/desktop` from "quick-wins applied" to "first public `@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0` release shipping a signed, notarized, auto-updating macOS binary with Sentry-readable stack traces." Six phases. Phase A is a human-setup checklist; B–F are code/CI changes. All four prior quick-win phases are already applied and verified in the current tree.

## Current State Analysis

All four phases of `2026-04-23-desktop-codex-gap-quick-wins.md` are landed:
- Entitlements trimmed (`build/entitlements.mac.plist` has 5 keys; over-broad `disable-library-validation`/`device.camera`/`network.server` removed).
- `forge.config.ts:67-78` has `NSQuitAlwaysKeepsWindows=false`, `LSMinimumSystemVersion=12.0`, `LSEnvironment.MallocNanoZone="0"`.
- `src/main/sentry.ts:14-55` has `SESSION_ID`, `rewriteFramesIntegration({root, prefix: "app:///"})`, `dist=buildNumber`, `bundle/host/sessionId` tags.
- `showContextMenu` IPC, `silentRefresh`, and the deep-link `console.log` are gone. `protocol.ts` deleted; OAuth moved to a loopback HTTP server (`auth-flow.ts:70-141`).

What still blocks a v0.1.0 release:
- `.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml.disabled` is inert (correctly, since Apple signing secrets aren't provisioned yet).
- `forge.config.ts:43-54` reads `LIGHTFAST_DESKTOP_RELEASE_REPO` but the disabled workflow passes `${{ github.repository }}` — two conflicting paths.
- `@sentry/cli` is in `pnpm-workspace.yaml:82` `onlyBuiltDependencies` but no script invokes it; packaged-build stack traces will be unreadable despite `rewriteFramesIntegration` being wired.
- `ci-core.yml:45,105` explicitly excludes desktop from typecheck + build. `ci.yml:50-54` only runs typecheck via `turbo --affected`. Nothing runs `electron-forge package` on PRs.
- `apps/desktop/package.json` placeholders: `version: "0.0.0"`, empty `sparkleFeedUrl`, unused `sparklePublicKey: ""`.
- No `.env.example`, no `lint`/`clean` scripts in desktop, `dev:desktop-stack` is misnamed at the root.

### Key Discoveries

- `sparklePublicKey` is declared in `src/shared/env.ts:12`, `src/shared/ipc.ts:52`, and `src/main/build-info.ts:23` but has **zero** downstream readers (grep confirms). Electron's `autoUpdater` does not use it. Drop the field instead of stamping it in CI.
- Sentry DSNs are public identifiers (they're visible in any shipped app's JS bundle). Baking at CI build time via `npm pkg set sentryDsn=...` from a secret is simplest — no runtime env plumbing required on macOS.
- Release tag convention in this repo is already `<package>@<version>` (changesets produces `lightfast@x.y.z`, `@lightfastai/mcp@x.y.z` via `release.yml`). Desktop should match: `@lightfast/desktop@<version>`. YAML trigger: `'@lightfast/desktop@*'`.
- Desktop is `"private": true`; `changesets/action` ignores private packages automatically — it cannot and will not try to publish desktop. The blocker isn't publishing, it's that `verify-changeset.yml:51` rejects any changeset file mentioning `@lightfast/desktop`. We stay tag-only and add the package to `.changeset/config.json` `ignore` so `pnpm changeset` never prompts for it.
- Monorepo is public (`gh repo view` → `"visibility":"PUBLIC"`). Sparkle feed URL `github.com/lightfastai/lightfast/releases/latest/download/latest-mac-${arch}.json` works without auth.
- `.vite/build` (main + preload) and `.vite/renderer/main_window` (renderer) are the two source-map roots `sentry-cli upload-sourcemaps --url-prefix "app:///"` needs to target — both bundles end up addressed via the `app:///` prefix by `rewriteFramesIntegration`.

## Desired End State

1. `git tag @lightfast/desktop@0.1.0 && git push origin '@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0'` fires `desktop-release.yml`, which builds signed+notarized arm64 and x64 `.zip` + `.dmg`, uploads source maps to Sentry, publishes GitHub release assets with Squirrel.Mac JSON feeds, and undrafts the release.
2. Packaged app's Sentry issues have fully symbolicated stacks (file/line → repo file/line), session tag, `dist`, and the `@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0+<runNumber>` release id.
3. Every PR touching `apps/desktop/**` runs typecheck + `electron-forge package` on macOS and fails if either breaks.
4. A new contributor runs `cp apps/desktop/.env.example apps/desktop/.env.development && pnpm dev:desktop` and the app boots.
5. `pnpm changeset` in the repo never prompts for `@lightfast/desktop`.

Verification:
- `gh release view '@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0' --repo lightfastai/lightfast` shows 6 assets (arm64 zip+dmg+json, x64 zip+dmg+json) plus undraft state.
- `sentry-cli releases info '@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0+<runNumber>' --project lightfast-desktop` lists uploaded source maps.
- `.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml` exists and is green on the tag push.
- `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop lint` passes.

## What We're NOT Doing

- Sparkle-native / Ed25519 auto-update (Squirrel.Mac is fine for v0.1.0). `sparklePublicKey` gets deleted, not stamped.
- Windows + Linux packaging (`MakerSquirrel` stays in `forge.config.ts:82-85` dormant; no Linux makers; no Windows job in the workflow).
- Universal binaries (`@electron/universal`). Two separate `-arm64` / `-x64` artifacts are published; the existing `${arch}` template in `updater.ts:27-29` handles dispatch.
- File-backed structured logger. Sentry + breadcrumbs cover v0.1.0; revisit once a user-issue-debugging workflow demands it.
- SQLite / persistent structured storage, worker tier, per-surface preload isolation, renderer i18n, Playwright harness, notifications — all deferred per original research `Status Tracker`.
- Wiring desktop into the changeset-driven release flow (`release.yml`). Desktop stays tag-only.
- A cross-repo `lightfastai/lightfast-desktop-releases` release host. Monorepo tags only.

## Implementation Approach

Strict phase order because of dependencies:

- **Phase A (setup)** must happen before D can be tested end-to-end. Runs in parallel with B/C/E/F code work.
- **Phase B** must land before D (D depends on Vite's DSN injection from B).
- **Phase C** adds the script that D calls.
- **Phases E and F** are independent — can land in either order relative to B–D.

### Prerequisites (outside this plan)

- `thoughts/shared/plans/2026-04-24-coderabbit-pr614-fixes.md` — resolve the port-collision and `will-navigate` guard items flagged against the loopback OAuth flow in PR #614 before Phase D's end-to-end validation. An unsigned release that crashes on a port conflict during sign-in will look worse than not releasing at all.

---

## Phase A: External Setup Checklist (human)

### Overview

No code. Provision the four external resources the workflow depends on, and commit secrets to GitHub. Skipping any item here will leave `desktop-release.yml` failing on the first run.

### Apple Developer (8 secrets)

1. Enroll in **Apple Developer Program** (~$99/yr) under the Lightfast business entity. Capture the 10-character **Team ID** once enrolled.
2. Generate a **Developer ID Application** certificate in developer.apple.com → Certificates. Download the `.cer`, add to Keychain Access on a Mac, export the matching identity as a password-protected `.p12`.
3. Generate an **App Store Connect API key** with "Developer" role (developer.apple.com → Users and Access → Keys). Download the `.p8` (one-time download). Capture the **Key ID** (10-char) and **Issuer ID** (UUID).
4. In repo settings → Secrets and variables → Actions, add these secrets:
   - `APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY` — full identity string, e.g. `Developer ID Application: Lightfast AI Inc (ABC1234567)`
   - `APPLE_TEAM_ID` — 10-char team id (e.g. `ABC1234567`)
   - `APPLE_CERT_BASE64` — output of `base64 < DeveloperID.p12 | pbcopy` (macOS) or `base64 -w0 DeveloperID.p12` (Linux)
   - `APPLE_CERT_PASSWORD` — the p12 password chosen at export
   - `APPLE_API_KEY_ID` — the 10-char key id from step 3
   - `APPLE_API_ISSUER` — the issuer UUID from step 3
   - `APPLE_API_KEY_CONTENT` — raw contents of the `.p8` file (including the `-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----` header/footer)
   - `KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD` — any strong random string (e.g. `openssl rand -base64 24`); used only inside the CI keychain

### Sentry (2 secrets + 1 project)

1. In Sentry, create a new **Electron** project named `lightfast-desktop` under the existing Lightfast org. Capture the DSN.
2. Create a Sentry **organization auth token** with `project:releases` scope (Settings → Developer Settings → Auth Tokens).
3. Add these secrets to the repo:
   - `SENTRY_DSN` — the DSN from step 1 (public, but storing as a secret keeps it out of git)
   - `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` — the auth token from step 2
4. Add these repo variables (Variables tab, not Secrets):
   - `SENTRY_ORG` — Sentry org slug (e.g. `lightfast`)
   - `SENTRY_PROJECT` — `lightfast-desktop`

### Success Criteria

Nothing to automate. Cross off the 11 items above; confirm by visiting `github.com/lightfastai/lightfast/settings/secrets/actions` and seeing all 8 Apple secrets + 2 Sentry secrets + 2 Sentry variables present.

**Stop here for human confirmation that Phase A is complete before starting Phase D validation.**

---

## Phase B: Env layer overhaul + forge config + sparkle cleanup

### Overview

Four cleanups, with the env layer as the load-bearing change:

1. **New t3-env-based env layer** at `apps/desktop/src/env/` — replaces the silent-fail `parseRuntimeEnv` in `src/shared/env.ts` with two `createEnv` calls (main, renderer). Matches the repo convention used by every other workspace.
2. **Sentry DSN baked via Vite `define` with a custom token** (`__SENTRY_DSN__`) — not via `process.env.SENTRY_DSN` replacement. Sentry's official Electron troubleshooting docs warn against replacing `process.*` keys in Vite `define` because it can break Sentry SDK internals. Custom token pattern avoids the conflict.
3. **Forge config simplification** — hardcode the release repo, remove the `LIGHTFAST_DESKTOP_RELEASE_REPO` indirection.
4. **Sparkle cleanup** — drop the unused `sparklePublicKey` field end-to-end.

**Spike verdict (CONFIRMED, 2026-04-24)**: `@t3-oss/env-core` (ESM-only) inlines cleanly into Forge's main-process CJS bundle at package time. `ssr.noExternal` is NOT needed — Forge's `@electron-forge/plugin-vite@7.11.1` bundles everything except electron + `node:*` builtins by default. Custom-token `define` works as expected. See Improvement Log.

**Why this matters now (not deferred)**: Today's `parseRuntimeEnv` silently returns `{}` on validation failure (`apps/desktop/src/shared/env.ts:26-31`). A packaged app with a missing/malformed env var boots with wrong defaults and Sentry silently doesn't initialize. This is the opposite of the "build-fail-fast" behavior that makes `apps/www/next.config.ts` safe. Shipping v0.1.0 with this behavior turns every future env-touching change into a landmine.

### Changes Required

#### 1. `apps/desktop/package.json` — add `@t3-oss/env-core`

**File**: `apps/desktop/package.json` (devDependencies, alphabetical insertion)
**Changes**: Add `"@t3-oss/env-core": "catalog:"` (already pinned to `^0.13.11` in `pnpm-workspace.yaml`). Run `pnpm install` at repo root to refresh `pnpm-lock.yaml`.

Rationale: every other workspace (`vendor/db`, `vendor/inngest`, `vendor/upstash`, `core/cli`, `db/app`, `packages/app-providers`) uses `@t3-oss/env-core` for non-Next.js contexts. Desktop is the only outlier with a hand-rolled schema. Adopting the catalog dep is one line.

#### 2. `apps/desktop/src/env/main.ts` (new file)

**File**: `apps/desktop/src/env/main.ts`
**Changes**: New file. Single source of truth for main-process env vars. Build-fail-fast via t3-env.

```ts
import { createEnv } from "@t3-oss/env-core";
import { z } from "zod";

// Populated by Vite's `define` at package time (see vite.main.config.ts).
// `undefined` guard covers `pnpm dev` (define is still applied) and
// ad-hoc `node` invocations during tooling.
declare const __SENTRY_DSN__: string | undefined;

const buildFlavor = z.enum(["dev", "preview", "prod"]);

export const mainEnv = createEnv({
  server: {
    // Sentry (baked at build time via Vite define; optional in dev).
    SENTRY_DSN: z.string().url().optional(),
    // API origin. Main-process side (auth-flow loopback, CSP allowlist).
    LIGHTFAST_API_URL: z
      .string()
      .url()
      .default("http://localhost:3024"),
    // Clerk frontend API — used by index.ts to compute the CSP `connect-src`
    // host for the Clerk iframe. Required (sign-in breaks otherwise).
    NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY: z
      .string()
      .min(1, "Missing NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY"),
    // Build-info overrides (used by build-info.ts).
    BUILD_FLAVOR: buildFlavor.optional(),
    SPARKLE_FEED_URL: z.string().url().optional(),
    SQUIRREL_FEED_URL: z.string().url().optional(),
    // Dev-only remote debugging.
    LIGHTFAST_REMOTE_DEBUG_PORT: z.coerce
      .number()
      .int()
      .min(1)
      .max(65535)
      .optional(),
  },
  runtimeEnv: {
    SENTRY_DSN:
      typeof __SENTRY_DSN__ !== "undefined" && __SENTRY_DSN__ !== ""
        ? __SENTRY_DSN__
        : process.env.SENTRY_DSN,
    LIGHTFAST_API_URL: process.env.LIGHTFAST_API_URL,
    NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY:
      process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY,
    BUILD_FLAVOR: process.env.BUILD_FLAVOR,
    SPARKLE_FEED_URL: process.env.SPARKLE_FEED_URL,
    SQUIRREL_FEED_URL: process.env.SQUIRREL_FEED_URL,
    LIGHTFAST_REMOTE_DEBUG_PORT: process.env.LIGHTFAST_REMOTE_DEBUG_PORT,
  },
  isServer: true,
  emptyStringAsUndefined: true,
  skipValidation: !!process.env.SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION,
});
```

Notes:
- `isServer: true` is explicit because t3-env's default heuristic (`typeof window === "undefined"`) misfires in Electron's preload context (preload has `window` but is Node-side).
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY` keeps its Next.js-style name because that's what the rest of the monorepo sets (`apps/app`, `apps/www`); renaming would fragment the contributor mental model. It's server-only in the desktop schema because only main reads it (for CSP).
- `default()` on `LIGHTFAST_API_URL` collapses the duplicated `getApiOrigin()` helpers in `index.ts:45-51` and `auth-flow.ts:10-16` into a single validated value.

**File**: `apps/desktop/src/env/renderer.ts`
**Changes**: New file. Renderer env validation with Vite's `VITE_*` prefix.

```ts

import { z } from "zod";

export const rendererEnv = createEnv({
  clientPrefix: "VITE_",
  client: {
    VITE_LIGHTFAST_API_URL: z
      .string()
      .url()
      .default("http://localhost:3024"),
  },
  runtimeEnv: import.meta.env,
  emptyStringAsUndefined: true,
  skipValidation: !!import.meta.env.VITE_SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION,
});
```

Only `VITE_LIGHTFAST_API_URL` is validated because that's the only `import.meta.env` access in the renderer tree today (`entry.tsx:7`). file.content:
name: Desktop CI

on:
  pull_request:
    paths:
      - 'apps/desktop/**'
      - 'packages/app-trpc/**'
      - 'packages/ui/**'
      - 'packages/lib/**'
      - 'pnpm-lock.yaml'
      - 'pnpm-workspace.yaml'
      - '.github/workflows/desktop-ci.yml'
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - 'apps/desktop/**'
      - 'packages/app-trpc/**'
      - 'packages/ui/**'
      - 'packages/lib/**'
      - 'pnpm-lock.yaml'
      - 'pnpm-workspace.yaml'
      - '.github/workflows/desktop-ci.yml'

concurrency:
  group: desktop-ci-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  package:
    name: Typecheck + package (unsigned)
    runs-on: macos-14
    timeout-minutes: 30
    env:
      TURBO_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.TURBO_TOKEN }}
      TURBO_TEAM: ${{ vars.TURBO_TEAM }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@b906affcce14559ad1aafd4ab0e942779e9f58b1 # v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: 'pnpm'

      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Typecheck
        run: pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck

      - name: Package (unsigned)
        run: pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop package

input.prompt:
Goal: produce a precise map of every place in `.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml` and `apps/desktop/forge.config.ts` that assumes Apple-signing secrets (APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY, APPLE_TEAM_ID, APPLE_CERT_BASE64, APPLE_CERT_PASSWORD, APPLE_API_KEY_ID, APPLE_API_ISSUER, APPLE_API_KEY_CONTENT, KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD) exist, so we can decide which steps to gate behind a conditional or skip entirely for an unsigned-beta release. We are NOT writing code — we are mapping the surface area.

Specifically document:

1. **For every step in `.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml`**: line numbers, name, what it does, and what happens if the secret it references is empty (does it hard-fail, no-op, succeed silently?). Especially: "Import Apple signing certificate" (lines 100-113), "Write notarize API key" (lines 115-123), and the actual `pnpm exec electron-forge publish` step (line 127). 
   - Will `security create-keychain -p "" build.keychain` succeed or fail with empty `KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD`?
   - Will `base64 -d > /tmp/cert.p12` succeed with empty `APPLE_CERT_BASE64`?
   - Will the keychain step fail and short-circuit the build, or will it succeed silently and leave the keychain empty?
   
2. **For `apps/desktop/forge.config.ts:14-41`**: confirm the gating is purely env-driven (osxSign/osxNotarize undefined when env missing), and document exactly what `electron-forge publish --arch=<arch> --platform=darwin` produces when osxSign/osxNotarize are both undefined. What's in the resulting `.dmg`/`.zip`? Does it have an ad-hoc signature, or no signature at all?

3. **For `apps/desktop/scripts/upload-sourcemaps.mjs`** and the Sentry steps: does the Sentry source-map upload step have any dependency on signing, or is it independent? Will it run cleanly on an unsigned build?

4. **For `apps/desktop/scripts/generate-update-feed.mjs`**: this generates `latest-mac-{arch}.json` pointing at the .zip. Does the feed generation care about signing? (Squirrel.Mac signature enforcement is a runtime client concern; the feed JSON itself shouldn't.)

5. **For `actions/attest-build-provenance@v2`** (line 133): does this work on unsigned artifacts? (My understanding: yes — provenance is independent of code signing.)

6. **What's the minimal set of workflow conditional changes** needed to make the workflow ship a fully unsigned build when secrets are absent? (e.g., wrap each Apple-touching step with `if: ${{ secrets.APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY != '' }}`, or add a job-level `unsigned: bool` input.)

Return a tight, well-organized doc with file:line citations. Document what IS — do not propose changes beyond enumerating the conditional surfaces. Under 600 words.

Research question: For an Electron 39 app distributed as an unsigned `.app` (no Apple Developer ID, no notarization, no codesign), what is the **runtime behavior** when the app:

1. **First launch**: What does macOS Gatekeeper do on macOS 12+ (Monterey through Sequoia / 15.x)? Specifically:
   - Quarantine attribute attached when downloading via browser (`.dmg` from GitHub releases)
   - "App is damaged and can't be opened" vs "Apple cannot check it for malicious software" warning text
   - Right-click → Open bypass: still works on Sequoia? Or removed? (My understanding: still works, but newer macOS may require Settings → Privacy & Security → "Open Anyway" instead)
   - Does `xattr -cr <App.app>` (clear quarantine) bypass Gatekeeper if user runs it? Is this a documented user workflow?

2. **Auto-update via Electron's built-in `autoUpdater`** (Squirrel.Mac):
   - Squirrel.Mac documentation says "your update must be code-signed for the auto-updater to work" — confirm this is enforced (signature verification before swap), or just a strong recommendation. What is the actual error if you call `autoUpdater.setFeedURL` + `checkForUpdates()` on an unsigned app?
   - Does the SHA1 vs Designated Requirement check matter here?
   - Is there a workaround (ad-hoc signing the same identity for old + new builds, or `--force`-style flags)?

3. **Ad-hoc signing as a middle path** (`codesign --sign -`):
   - Does ad-hoc signing satisfy Squirrel.Mac's signature-match check? (My understanding: no — Squirrel.Mac requires the new build's signature to match the running app's signature, and ad-hoc identity is "-" which technically matches "-", so it might work for self-update of ad-hoc to ad-hoc, but won't allow upgrading from ad-hoc to a real Developer ID later.)
   - Does ad-hoc signing change the Gatekeeper UX? (No — still triggers "unidentified developer" because not from Apple-trusted root.)
   - Is `electron-forge` / `@electron/osx-sign` configurable for ad-hoc signing? (Yes — pass `identity: "-"`. But hardened-runtime + ad-hoc has known issues.)

4. **Migration from unsigned beta → signed v0.1.0**:
   - Confirmed: Squirrel.Mac will refuse to swap binaries because the new signature doesn't match. So users on unsigned beta need a manual reinstall (download new .dmg, drag to Applications). Verify this is the consensus approach.
   - Any "graceful migration" tricks documented (e.g., self-uninstalling the old build, prompting users with a "Update available — please reinstall manually" UI)?

5. **Other related considerations**:
   - Does Electron 39's hardened-runtime requirement interact with unsigned builds? (Hardened runtime is part of signing — without signing, no hardened runtime, so entitlements don't matter for unsigned.)
   - Any UX/legal/distribution concern with shipping an unsigned macOS app? (e.g., Anthropic Claude.app, Linear, Figma — did any ship unsigned in early days, and what was the experience?)
   - GitHub Releases: any restrictions on hosting unsigned `.dmg` or `.zip`?

Cite official docs (Apple Developer, Electron docs, Squirrel.Mac repo, electron-forge docs) and reputable community sources. Confidence-calibrated — flag any claims you're under 80% sure of. Under 800 words.

input.description: Find install-UX patterns for unsigned macOS apps
input.prompt:
Research question: For shipping an unsigned macOS Electron app to a closed beta of ~10-100 developer-y users on macOS 12+, what's the modern UX/copy/instruction pattern that other small startups use?

Cover:

1. **The Gatekeeper bypass instruction**: What's the exact, current (2025-2026) flow on Apple Silicon Macs running macOS 14/15? My understanding:
   - User downloads `.dmg` from a GitHub release URL
   - User opens `.dmg` and drags app to Applications
   - User double-clicks the app → "Lightfast can't be opened because Apple cannot check it for malicious software"
   - User goes to System Settings → Privacy & Security → scrolls to bottom → "Open Anyway" button next to the blocked app
   - Confirm this is correct. Note any deviations on Sequoia (15.x) where Apple has tightened this.

2. **Right-click → Open trick**: Does this still work on Sequoia? (Apple removed the right-click bypass for some Gatekeeper modes; right-click → Open is still effective for "unidentified developer" but NOT for "broken seal" / "damaged" apps that have been quarantined post-download.)

3. **`xattr -cr` recipe**: Some open-source projects publish a one-line shell command users run after install (`xattr -cr /Applications/Lightfast.app`) to clear the quarantine attribute. Pros/cons. Is this still effective on macOS 15? Risks of asking non-technical users to paste shell commands?

4. **README/landing-page copy patterns**: Find 3-5 real-world examples of small startups or open-source projects that ship unsigned macOS Electron/Tauri/Swift apps and look at how they document the install. Examples to check:
   - Raycast (early days?)
   - Linear (early days?)
   - Beeper / Texts.com
   - Any indie Electron apps on GitHub with > 1k stars
   - Tauri example apps
   
   For each, capture the actual copy they use to walk a user through the Gatekeeper warning. We want voice/tone benchmarks.

5. **Closed-beta delivery channel**: For a 10-100 user beta, do startups usually:
   - Drop a public GitHub release with download links and the bypass instructions
   - Use a private Discord/Slack channel with the .dmg file
   - Use TestFlight-equivalent (there is no equivalent for Mac outside the App Store)
   - Use a third-party tool like Tailscale's beta channel, Vercel, etc.
   - Direct DMs?

6. **Communicating "this is a beta, you'll need to reinstall when we get our cert"**: Any examples of this kind of "transitional unsigned build" comms? How do they set expectations?

7. **Risk: what could go wrong?** Specifically:
   - Gatekeeper getting more restrictive in macOS 16 (rumored / public roadmap)
   - Users getting confused by the warning and assuming the app is malware → bad reputation
   - Users running `xattr` and getting confused
   - Antivirus software (Malwarebytes, etc.) flagging unsigned apps more aggressively

Return a tight document with concrete URLs and copy snippets. Confidence-calibrated. Under 700 words.

content.text:
## Analysis: Apple-Signing Surface Area

### 1. `.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml` — Step-by-Step Map

**Lines 66-75 — Job-level `env` block**
Four Apple secrets land as env vars for the entire `build` job: `APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY`, `APPLE_TEAM_ID`, `APPLE_API_KEY_ID`, `APPLE_API_ISSUER`. When the secrets are absent, GitHub Actions injects an **empty string** for each. No step fails here; they are just promoted to the runner environment.

---

**Lines 100-113 — "Import Apple signing certificate"**
This is the first hard-fail surface. The shell commands execute unconditionally:

- Line 106: `echo "$APPLE_CERT_BASE64" | base64 -d > /tmp/cert.p12`
  With an empty `APPLE_CERT_BASE64`, `base64 -d` receives an empty string. On macOS, `base64 -d` with empty input writes a **zero-byte file** to `/tmp/cert.p12` and exits 0 — it does NOT fail.

- Line 107: `security create-keychain -p "$KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD" build.keychain`
  `KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD` expands to `""`. `security create-keychain -p ""` is **valid** — an empty password is accepted. Exits 0.

- Lines 108-110: `security default-keychain`, `security unlock-keychain -p "" build.keychain`, `security set-keychain-settings` — all succeed with an empty password.

- Line 111: `security import /tmp/cert.p12 -k build.keychain -P "$APPLE_CERT_PASSWORD" -T /usr/bin/codesign`
  Importing a **zero-byte** `.p12` file. `security import` will exit **non-zero** here ("The specified item could not be found" or "Unable to decode the file"). **This step hard-fails the job.**

- Line 113: `rm /tmp/cert.p12` — never reached if line 111 fails.

---

**Lines 115-123 — "Write notarize API key"**
Executes unconditionally. With `APPLE_API_KEY_CONTENT` empty:
- Line 121: `key_file` path contains the empty string for `APPLE_API_KEY_ID_SECRET`, producing `$HOME/.private_keys/AuthKey_.p8`.
- Line 122: `printf '%s' ""` writes a **zero-byte file** at that path. Exits 0.
- Line 123: `APPLE_API_KEY` is set in `GITHUB_ENV` to that path.

This step **succeeds silently** with empty secrets, leaving an empty `.p8` file and a well-formed but useless `APPLE_API_KEY` env var.

---

**Line 127 — "Publish (forge)" (`pnpm exec electron-forge publish`)**
Reads `osxSign` and `osxNotarize` from `forge.config.ts` (see section 2 below). If the keychain step at line 111 did not fail, and if both `APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY`/`APPLE_TEAM_ID` are empty, `osxSign` is `undefined` and electron-forge skips codesigning entirely. The build produces an **unsigned** `.dmg` and `.zip`. If the keychain step did fail, this step is never reached.

---

**Line 131 — "Upload source maps to Sentry"**
Fully independent of signing. See section 3.

---

**Lines 133-136 — `actions/attest-build-provenance@v2`**
Runs unconditionally after forge publish. Operates on `apps/desktop/out/make/**/*.zip`. Provenance attestation is a GitHub-side operation — it does not inspect or require code signatures inside the artifact. Succeeds on unsigned zips.

---

### 2. `apps/desktop/forge.config.ts` — Env-Gated Signing

**Lines 14-30 — `osxSign` guard**
`osxSign` is assigned a signing config object only when **both** `process.env.APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY` and `process.env.APPLE_TEAM_ID` are non-empty truthy strings. If either is empty/absent, `osxSign` is `undefined`.

**Lines 32-41 — `osxNotarize` guard**
`osxNotarize` is assigned only when **all three** of `APPLE_API_KEY`, `APPLE_API_KEY_ID`, and `APPLE_API_ISSUER` are truthy. `APPLE_API_KEY` is the path written to `GITHUB_ENV` at workflow line 123 — it is always set to *something* by the "Write notarize API key" step (even the path to an empty file). In that case the string is truthy, so notarization would be attempted. However, if `APPLE_API_KEY_ID` is also empty, the path itself becomes `AuthKey_.p8` and `APPLE_API_KEY_ID` in the env is empty, making the three-way AND false. `osxNotarize` then resolves to `undefined`.

**Lines 60-61 — Spread into `packagerConfig`**
Both are spread conditionally: `...(osxSign && { osxSign })` and `...(osxNotarize && { osxNotarize })`. When both are `undefined`, `packagerConfig` has no signing config at all.

**What electron-forge produces with both undefined:** `MakerZIP` (line 81) and `MakerDMG` (line 82) run without codesigning. The resulting `.app` bundle inside the `.zip` and `.dmg` carries **no Apple Developer signature and no ad-hoc signature** — it is completely unsigned. macOS Gatekeeper will block launch on any machine with default settings.

---

### 3. `apps/desktop/scripts/upload-sourcemaps.mjs` — Sentry Upload

**Lines 13-19** — Guards only on `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN`, `SENTRY_ORG`, `SENTRY_PROJECT`. Exits 1 immediately if any are absent. No reference to any Apple secret. The upload reads compiled JS/map files from `.vite/build` and `.vite/renderer/main_window` — directories produced by electron-forge's Vite plugin during the package step regardless of signing. **Fully independent of signing.**

---

### 4. `apps/desktop/scripts/generate-update-feed.mjs` — Update Feed

**Lines 7-18** — Guards on `GH_REPO`, `RELEASE_TAG`, `RELEASE_VERSION` only. No Apple secret references anywhere in the file. Feed generation calls `gh release view` to get the list of uploaded assets, finds a `.zip` matching `darwin-{arch}`, and writes `latest-mac-{arch}.json` with `url`, `name`, `notes`, `pub_date` fields. The JSON contains only a download URL — it carries **no signature data** and is agnostic to whether the artifact is signed. Squirrel.Mac signature enforcement happens at client runtime, not in feed generation.

---

### 5. Conditional Surface Map — Minimal Gate Points

There are exactly **two steps** that touch Apple secrets and have no existing `if:` guard:

| Location | Step name | Current `if:` | What needs gating |
|---|---|---|---|
| Line 100-113 | "Import Apple signing certificate" | none | Entire step |
| Line 115-123 | "Write notarize API key" | none | Entire step |

All other Apple-touching behavior (signing, notarizing) is already env-driven inside `forge.config.ts` lines 14-41 — if the env vars are empty, those code paths produce `undefined` and forge skips them automatically.

The single conditional expression that would gate both shell steps is checking `secrets.APPLE_CERT_BASE64 != ''` (or `APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY != ''`) on each step's `if:` field. No other steps require structural changes; forge's own guards handle the rest.

- Lines 108-110: `security default-keychain`, `security unlock-keychain -p "" build.keychain`, `security set-keychain-settings` — all succeed with an empty password.

- Line 111: `security import /tmp/cert.p12 -k build.keychain -P "$APPLE_CERT_PASSWORD" -T /usr/bin/codesign`
  Importing a **zero-byte** `.p12` file. `security import` will exit **non-zero** here ("The specified item could not be found" or "Unable to decode the file"). **This step hard-fails the job.**

- Line 113: `rm /tmp/cert.p12` — never reached if line 111 fails.

---

**Lines 115-123 — "Write notarize API key"**
Executes unconditionally. With `APPLE_API_KEY_CONTENT` empty:
- Line 121: `key_file` path contains the empty string for `APPLE_API_KEY_ID_SECRET`, producing `$HOME/.private_keys/AuthKey_.p8`.
- Line 122: `printf '%s' ""` writes a **zero-byte file** at that path. Exits 0.
- Line 123: `APPLE_API_KEY` is set in `GITHUB_ENV` to that path.

This step **succeeds silently** with empty secrets, leaving an empty `.p8` file and a well-formed but useless `APPLE_API_KEY` env var.

---

**Line 127 — "Publish (forge)" (`pnpm exec electron-forge publish`)**
Reads `osxSign` and `osxNotarize` from `forge.config.ts` (see section 2 below). If the keychain step at line 111 did not fail, and if both `APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY`/`APPLE_TEAM_ID` are empty, `osxSign` is `undefined` and electron-forge skips codesigning entirely. The build produces an **unsigned** `.dmg` and `.zip`. If the keychain step did fail, this step is never reached.

---

**Line 131 — "Upload source maps to Sentry"**
Fully independent of signing. See section 3.

---

**Lines 133-136 — `actions/attest-build-provenance@v2`**
Runs unconditionally after forge publish. Operates on `apps/desktop/out/make/**/*.zip`. Provenance attestation is a GitHub-side operation — it does not inspect or require code signatures inside the artifact. Succeeds on unsigned zips.

---

### 2. `apps/desktop/forge.config.ts` — Env-Gated Signing

**Lines 14-30 — `osxSign` guard**
`osxSign` is assigned a signing config object only when **both** `process.env.APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY` and `process.env.APPLE_TEAM_ID` are non-empty truthy strings. If either is empty/absent, `osxSign` is `undefined`.

**Lines 32-41 — `osxNotarize` guard**
`osxNotarize` is assigned only when **all three** of `APPLE_API_KEY`, `APPLE_API_KEY_ID`, and `APPLE_API_ISSUER` are truthy. `APPLE_API_KEY` is the path written to `GITHUB_ENV` at workflow line 123 — it is always set to *something* by the "Write notarize API key" step (even the path to an empty file). In that case the string is truthy, so notarization would be attempted. However, if `APPLE_API_KEY_ID` is also empty, the path itself becomes `AuthKey_.p8` and `APPLE_API_KEY_ID` in the env is empty, making the three-way AND false. `osxNotarize` then resolves to `undefined`.

**Lines 60-61 — Spread into `packagerConfig`**
Both are spread conditionally: `...(osxSign && { osxSign })` and `...(osxNotarize && { osxNotarize })`. When both are `undefined`, `packagerConfig` has no signing config at all.

**What electron-forge produces with both undefined:** `MakerZIP` (line 81) and `MakerDMG` (line 82) run without codesigning. The resulting `.app` bundle inside the `.zip` and `.dmg` carries **no Apple Developer signature and no ad-hoc signature** — it is completely unsigned. macOS Gatekeeper will block launch on any machine with default settings.

---

### 3. `apps/desktop/scripts/upload-sourcemaps.mjs` — Sentry Upload

**Lines 13-19** — Guards only on `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN`, `SENTRY_ORG`, `SENTRY_PROJECT`. Exits 1 immediately if any are absent. No reference to any Apple secret. The upload reads compiled JS/map files from `.vite/build` and `.vite/renderer/main_window` — directories produced by electron-forge's Vite plugin during the package step regardless of signing. **Fully independent of signing.**

---

### 4. `apps/desktop/scripts/generate-update-feed.mjs` — Update Feed

**Lines 7-18** — Guards on `GH_REPO`, `RELEASE_TAG`, `RELEASE_VERSION` only. No Apple secret references anywhere in the file. Feed generation calls `gh release view` to get the list of uploaded assets, finds a `.zip` matching `darwin-{arch}`, and writes `latest-mac-{arch}.json` with `url`, `name`, `notes`, `pub_date` fields. The JSON contains only a download URL — it carries **no signature data** and is agnostic to whether the artifact is signed. Squirrel.Mac signature enforcement happens at client runtime, not in feed generation.

---

### 5. Conditional Surface Map — Minimal Gate Points

There are exactly **two steps** that touch Apple secrets and have no existing `if:` guard:

| Location | Step name | Current `if:` | What needs gating |
|---|---|---|---|
| Line 100-113 | "Import Apple signing certificate" | none | Entire step |
| Line 115-123 | "Write notarize API key" | none | Entire step |

All other Apple-touching behavior (signing, notarizing) is already env-driven inside `forge.config.ts` lines 14-41 — if the env vars are empty, those code paths produce `undefined` and forge skips them automatically.

The single conditional expression that would gate both shell steps is checking `secrets.APPLE_CERT_BASE64 != ''` (or `APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY != ''`) on each step's `if:` field. No other steps require structural changes; forge's own guards handle the rest.

## Analysis: Electron 39 Unsigned macOS App — Runtime Behavior, Gatekeeper, Squirrel.Mac Auto-Update, Ad-hoc Signing

### Summary

An unsigned Electron 39 app distributed via GitHub Releases as a `.dmg` will be quarantined by the browser, triggering Gatekeeper on first launch with a dialog that varies by architecture and signing state. On macOS Sequoia (15.x), the Control-click bypass is gone; users must go to System Settings > Privacy & Security. Squirrel.Mac's requirement for a code signature is enforced at the framework level in C (it throws an uncaught `NSException` in release builds), making auto-update completely non-functional for unsigned apps. Ad-hoc signing (`codesign --sign -`) is an architecturally-required middle path on Apple Silicon, and it does produce a designated requirement — meaning ad-hoc-to-ad-hoc updates can theoretically work. Migration from an unsigned/ad-hoc beta to a properly signed release requires a manual reinstall; no graceful in-app migration path is documented or implemented in Squirrel.Mac.

---

### Key Findings

#### 1. Gatekeeper First-Launch Behavior on macOS 12–15

**Confidence: High**

**Claim:** Browsers attach `com.apple.quarantine` to `.dmg` downloads. On first launch of a quarantined, unsigned app, Gatekeeper shows one of two dialogs depending on signing state. The dialog wording differs meaningfully.

**Quote:** "When you install Mac apps, plug-ins, and installer packages from outside the App Store, macOS checks the Developer ID signature to verify that the software is from an identified developer and that it has not been altered." — https://support.apple.com/en-us/102445

**Quote:** (Apple Support HT202491) "Alert that Apple cannot check the app for malicious software" — shown when the app has no Developer ID or notarization ticket. "Alert that the app will damage your computer or the app is damaged" — shown when macOS detects the signature is structurally invalid (broken, not merely absent).

**Provenance:** Apple Support doc (Technote 102445) + Apple Developer forums thread 740728 + eclectic light analysis (Oct 2024).

**Architecture split (important):**
- **x86_64 (Intel):** An unsigned or completely unsigned app shows "Apple cannot check it for malicious software" (the "unidentified developer" family). The app is blocked but the error is recoverable via Privacy & Security > Open Anyway.
- **arm64 (Apple Silicon):** macOS *requires* a valid code signature on arm64 executables because the CPU architecture mandate means every ARM64 Mach-O must be sealed. If the build process leaves a partial/invalid signature (common with electron-builder on ARM without an explicit certificate), macOS reports **"app is damaged and can't be opened"** — not the recoverable unidentified-developer dialog. This is a structural difference.

**Quote from electron-builder PR #9007 (April 2025):** "If no signing identity is present while an app is built and it is downloaded via a web browser, macOS on ARM shows 'app is damaged' errors that are hard to bypass for the average user... It is thus an error to not manually codesign the resulting app, even if no stable signing identity is available (in which case it must be an ad-hoc signature)." — https://github.com/electron-userland/electron-builder/pull/9007

#### 2. Sequoia (15.x): Control-click Bypass Removed

**Confidence: High** (Apple official announcement, confirmed by multiple secondary sources)

**Claim:** In macOS Sequoia, Control-click to override Gatekeeper is fully removed. Users must navigate to System Settings > Privacy & Security and click "Open Anyway," requiring an admin password.

**Quote:** "In macOS Sequoia, users will no longer be able to Control-click to override Gatekeeper when opening software that isn't signed correctly or notarized. They'll need to visit System Settings > Privacy & Security to review security information for software before allowing it to run." — https://developer.apple.com/news/?id=saqachfa (Apple Developer News, August 6, 2024)

**Provenance:** Official Apple Developer News announcement → confirmed by Apple Support Technote 102445 (HT202491).

**Note:** Technote 102445 still shows the Privacy & Security "Open Anyway" path as the supported user workflow. The `xattr -cr <App.app>` approach (removing the quarantine attribute) still works as a workaround, removes all Gatekeeper checks, and is widely documented as a developer-facing workaround — but it is not a consumer-safe user flow.

#### 3. Squirrel.Mac Signature Enforcement: Hard Requirement, Not Recommendation

**Confidence: High** (verified against source code)

**Claim:** Squirrel.Mac's signature check is enforced in code, not advisory. In a release build, attempting to initialize `SQRLUpdater` without a code signature throws an `NSException` that crashes the app. In debug builds, it returns `nil` and logs a message.

**Quote from `SQRLUpdater.m` (Squirrel.Mac source, latest commit):**
```objc
_signature = [SQRLCodeSignature currentApplicationSignature:&error];
if (_signature == nil) {
#if DEBUG
    NSLog(@"Could not get code signature for running application, application updates are disabled: %@", error);
    return nil;
#else
    NSDictionary *exceptionInfo = @{ NSUnderlyingErrorKey: error };
    @throw [NSException exceptionWithName:NSInternalInconsistencyException 
        reason:@"Could not get code signature for running application" 
        userInfo:exceptionInfo];
#endif
}
```
— https://github.com/Squirrel/Squirrel.Mac/blob/bde5ff2983e91e7310c4139223ed04870e14a5b1/Squirrel/SQRLUpdater.m

**Mechanism:** `currentApplicationSignature` calls `SecCodeCopySelf` then `SecCodeCopyDesignatedRequirement`. Apple's docs confirm: "No designated requirement can be obtained from unsigned code." — https://developer.apple.com/documentation/security/seccodecopydesignatedrequirement. So on a completely unsigned app, `SecCodeCopyDesignatedRequirement` returns a non-`noErr` status, `signatureWithCode:error:` returns `nil`, and `SQRLUpdater` init throws in release mode.

**Electron surfaces this as:** `Error: Could not get code signature for running application` — confirmed in multiple GitHub issues (electron/electron#8204, electron/electron#7476, electron/electron#5008).

**The Squirrel maintainer's own words:** "This behavior is provided/required by Squirrel which [is] the auto update library that Electron uses, it wasn't an explicit choice for Electron apps to have this requirement... I'm going to close this out since this would require major Squirrel.Mac changes." — https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/5008

#### 4. Ad-hoc Signing and Squirrel.Mac Compatibility

**Confidence: Medium** (logic follows from source code + Apple docs; no direct test of ad-hoc-to-ad-hoc Squirrel update found in authoritative sources)

**Claim:** Ad-hoc signing (`codesign --sign -`) produces a designated requirement via `SecCodeCopyDesignatedRequirement`, but that DR is tied to the specific binary content of that build. The update validation check in `SQRLInstaller.m` uses `SecStaticCodeCheckValidityWithErrors` against the running app's DR. For an ad-hoc signed update to pass, the new binary's content must satisfy the running app's DR — which for ad-hoc means they'd need to be the same binary. In practice, a new version of the app will have different binary content, making ad-hoc-to-ad-hoc updates via Squirrel non-functional.

**Apple docs confirm the mechanism:** "Ad hoc signed code... has a DR but it's tied to that specific version of the code." — https://developer.apple.com/documentation/technotes/tn3127-inside-code-signing-requirements

**Apple Code Signing Guide confirms:** "An ad-hoc signature does not use or record a cryptographic identity, and thus identifies exactly and only the one program being signed." — https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/Security/Conceptual/CodeSigningGuide/RequirementLang/RequirementLang.html

**Gatekeeper UX with ad-hoc:** Ad-hoc signing does NOT satisfy Gatekeeper. The app will still show an "unidentified developer" dialog (or "damaged" without quarantine bypass on arm64) because ad-hoc is not a Developer ID. However, it solves the arm64 "damaged" UX by providing a structurally valid signature, downgrading the error to the recoverable "Open Anyway" flow.

**electron-builder PR #9007 confirms:** Ad-hoc (`identity: "-"`) is now the recommended fallback for arm64 unsigned builds, with the specific goal of changing the "damaged" error to the more user-friendly "unidentified developer" flow. Merged April 2025.

#### 5. Migration: Unsigned/Ad-hoc Beta → Signed v0.1.0

**Confidence: High**

**Claim:** There is no graceful automatic migration path. Squirrel.Mac will fail the designated requirement check when the update's signing identity differs from the running app's (ad-hoc or unsigned). This means users must manually download and reinstall.

**Squirrel.Mac issue #160 (maintainer statement):** "For an update to be considered valid it must meet the designated requirement of the current version. This is done to verify the authenticity of a given update... If you've changed the certificate your application is signed by it is likely now not meeting the designated requirement of the old versions." — https://github.com/Squirrel/Squirrel.Mac/issues/160

The maintainer documents a "pivot update" approach for certificate rotation (ship a transitional build signed with old cert that expands the DR to include both old and new). This is NOT applicable to unsigned-to-signed migration because there is no old cert to sign the pivot with.

**Consensus approach:** Ship an in-app notification ("Update available — please download the new version") that opens the GitHub release page. No documented trick exists for self-uninstalling an unsigned Electron app on macOS in the way Windows NSIS allows.

#### 6. Hardened Runtime Interaction

**Confidence: High**

**Claim:** Hardened runtime is a signing-time property. Without code signing, hardened runtime is not applied and entitlements have no effect. This is not an additional constraint on unsigned apps — it simply doesn't apply.

**Provenance:** Apple notarization docs require hardened runtime as a prerequisite for notarization, not for running unsigned apps.

#### 7. GitHub Releases: No Restrictions on Unsigned DMGs

**Confidence: High**

GitHub imposes no code-signing requirements on release asset hosting. Unsigned `.dmg` and `.zip` files are routinely distributed via GitHub Releases by many projects (WebCord, ZAP, Headlamp, etc.) — all documented in the GitHub issues cited above.

---

### Source Chain

1. https://developer.apple.com/news/?id=saqachfa — Official Apple Developer announcement, August 6, 2024 (Sequoia Gatekeeper change)
2. https://support.apple.com/en-us/102445 — Apple Support Technote 102445 (Safely open apps on your Mac), official docs
3. https://github.com/Squirrel/Squirrel.Mac/blob/bde5ff2983e91e7310c4139223ed04870e14a5b1/Squirrel/SQRLUpdater.m — Squirrel.Mac source code, ground truth on enforcement
4. https://github.com/Squirrel/Squirrel.Mac/blob/e9e2188cda3efb4bc08b1719bdef71880f9dc9b1/Squirrel/SQRLCodeSignature.m — Squirrel.Mac source code, signature validation logic
5. https://developer.apple.com/documentation/security/seccodecopydesignatedrequirement — Apple Security framework docs (unsigned code has no DR)
6. https://developer.apple.com/documentation/technotes/tn3127-inside-code-signing-requirements — Apple TN3127, DR behavior for ad-hoc
7. https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/Security/Conceptual/CodeSigningGuide/RequirementLang/RequirementLang.html — Apple Code Signing Guide, ad-hoc semantics
8. https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/api/auto-updater — Electron official docs (signing required statement)
9. https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/5008 — Electron GitHub issue, maintainer confirmation of hard enforcement
10. https://github.com/Squirrel/Squirrel.Mac/issues/160 — Squirrel.Mac issue, maintainer statement on DR mismatch and pivot approach
11. https://github.com/electron-userland/electron-builder/pull/9007 — electron-builder PR #9007 (April 2025), ad-hoc signing as ARM64 fallback
12. https://eclecticlight.co/2024/10/01/living-without-notarization/ — Howard Oakley (The Eclectic Light Company), authoritative macOS security analysis, October 2024

---

### Conflicts

- The Apple Support page (HT202491) references "Alert that Apple cannot check the app for malicious software" as the standard dialog for unidentified developers, implying a single consistent experience. But multiple GitHub issues and the electron-builder PR #9007 (April 2025) document that arm64 macOS shows "app is damaged" instead of the standard dialog for apps without a valid signature — a stricter outcome. The Apple Support doc doesn't explicitly distinguish by architecture. The source code and community evidence are consistent: arm64 requires a valid signature at a lower level than Gatekeeper, making the "damaged" error the observed behavior there, not just a Gatekeeper policy outcome.

- Squirrel.Mac issue #179 has a community comment implying that updating will fail if binaries are not signed. The SQRLUpdater.m source shows the failure happens at init time (before `checkForUpdates` is even called), not at update download time. The source code is more precise.

---

### Gaps

- Whether ad-hoc-to-ad-hoc Squirrel.Mac updates actually work in practice has not been confirmed by any authoritative test. The logical analysis from the source code and Apple's DR semantics strongly suggests they would not (because ad-hoc DRs are content-specific), but no direct test result was found. This remains **uncertain**.
- No documented instance of a major app (Linear, Figma, etc.) shipping unsigned in early days was found. These are all closed-source with no accessible early release history.
- The behavior of `xattr -cr` specifically on macOS 15.4+ is noted to have new edge cases (Michael Tsai blog, July 2025) related to quarantine re-application by AppKit, but this applies to document files, not app bundles.

---

### Local Relevance

The desktop app release workflow (`desktop-release.yml disabled`, per MEMORY.md) is currently blocked on Apple signing keys. The above analysis directly confirms that shipping a unsigned arm64 build without at minimum ad-hoc signing will produce "app is damaged" errors that are not recoverable via right-click on Sequoia — which is the primary macOS version for Apple Silicon users. Ad-hoc signing is the minimum viable state for a distributed beta. Auto-update via Squirrel.Mac is non-functional without a Developer ID certificate regardless of ad-hoc status.
